messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following EdTech platform terms of service data section for GDPR compliance:

"EduLearn may collect information about how students interact with our platform including time spent on tasks, quiz scores, and video watch history. Teachers and school administrators can access this data. We use this to generate learning analytics reports. Data is stored on US servers. Schools are responsible for obtaining consent from parents where required."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated by EduLearn. Passing consent responsibility to schools does not constitute a lawful basis statement by EduLearn as processor or controller.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Learning difficulty or disability inferences can be drawn from performance data. No safeguards addressed.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Learning analytics is stated. Whether this extends to product improvement, advertising, or profiling is not clarified.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Video watch history may be disproportionate for learning analytics. Scope of data collection is not clearly bounded.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [FAIL] No retention period stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No data subject rights stated. Parental rights on behalf of children are not addressed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Teachers and school administrators named as internal recipients. No third-party processors disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] US servers disclosed but no transfer safeguard mechanism stated.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Processing of children's educational data at scale likely requires a DPO.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (no lawful basis), Art. 8 (children's data — parental consent mechanism not described), Art. 5 (no retention period, unclear collection scope), Art. 15-21 (no rights), Art. 28 (no processor disclosure), Art. 46 (US transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (DPO likely required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State EduLearn's own lawful basis for processing rather than delegating to schools
- Clarify controller/processor roles between EduLearn and schools
- Define the exact scope of data collected and justify each element
- Add a retention period
- Add a data subject and parental rights section
- Disclose all sub-processors
- Reference US transfer safeguards (SCCs)
- Appoint and disclose DPO
- Add breach notification commitment
